mechanics.text:
BY the NuMBERS
Kill him:" Then, as if remembering the order for minimal violence: Or knock him coid V'm in the rafters over the bay now; working my way across to the Omni: Reaching up, ipped one hand over the wire mic_ Why are you here?' asked Erik. Stroud s OmniMech is set up for the Clan direct enhanced: imaging interface _ He wants to try some experimental surgery: fitting me with the necessary El neural implant better than using neurohelmet, but apparently turns Your brain to jelly after few years And you cut the low card? Gray   nodded tne unconscious men_ volunteered; Apparently Stroud slipped clause into my Iast contract rene gotiation that allows him to make the decision for me_ The trial operation iS set for morning; There wasn time for this; with each pulse knew the num bers were bleeding away: But then again_ we were sent to dis rupt Skye Tiger's operations_ and sometimes you have to Iisten to your gut: unclasped the mic_ coming with told Stephen_ ~We be in the hangar in sec. nodded t0 Erik and swung the laser rifle out of Iine with his midsection. If you re coming move . He started forward again but brought himself up short and nodded toward unconscious guard: Can get gun? No. And stay where can see You, was willing to give the man chance wasn about t0 take him completely at face value As we passed through the room where he had been confined, he grabbed his neurohelmet from off chair and pulled over his head. Then he fished under his cot for his cooling vest something all MechWarriors relied on in the blistering heat of BattleMech cockpit and pulled that on as well: Good choices since both were resistant bullets and the guards around here seemed fond of the Rorynex. On the game world, you can only buck the odds so long then they land on you with their full weight When happened _ were halfway across the bay; sandwiched between partial: disassembled Atlas and the pristine Man War OmniMech_ The prize was SO new that Stroud had yet t0 remove the Ghost Bear insignia emblazoned across the right eg, though his own Skye Tiger emblem was prominent enough the chest; Stephen skulked the catwalks twenty meters overhead near- invisible shadow in the rafters Jaime led, while brought up the rear and kept Erik in sight The alarms rang out shrilly; echoing in the immense bay: laser trip beam on the floor? Or had patrol found any one of the five sleeping guards we'd left along our path? Not that really mattered one way or the other. 'We're madel yelled out; running for pile of heavy  plastic crates stacked near the OmniMech: The Man 0 War towered over us like some armored giant: Twelve meters tall; it commanded the bay just as would any battlefield or one of the Solaris arenas. Fortunately; it was miss: ing MechWarrior. Without someone like Erik in the cockpit, the machine was only SO much cold metal wrapped around damp
ened fusion engine. Erik and took cover behind the crates at just the right time Four guards burst through the same door we had used t0 enter the bay; SMGs already chattering ad slinging about deadly hail of lead. ricochet from the leg of the Omni sang past my ear and gouged into the plastic side of one box. edged out around the crate twelve-centimeter autocan: non ammunition by the stenciling and squeezed off - two shots with my pulse laser rifle. spray of ruby darts crossed the bay floor in the blink of an eye slamming into the right shoulder of one guard_ went down; weapon lost, and writhed about clasping his left hand over the charred ruin of his shoulder: Two nearby guards dove behind the detached Atlas arm and began working their way closer while keeping cover behind various parts and equipment; Then recalled that the MechWarrior was not in my sight: spun around  only to find him hunkered down behind the boxes, out of harm' way: He looked calm enough, catching my eye and offering shrug Jaime kneeled next to him_ arms extended in classic firing position as she fired her Nakjama over the top of their cover. crabbed over and relieved her of the gun The num- bers were slipping away fast. Too fast. Get that door open_ jerked my chin at the heavy metal door only five meters past the Omni. Fifteen meters high and ten wide it took up large portion of the wall and was one of two that could be used bring Mechs into the bay: It was also our closest avenue of escape  and Jaime could work on under par cover from the crates and the massive feet of the Man 0 War also knew would have the tightest security yet; testa ment t0 the priorities of mankind: The estate was warded with good security; but the measures in place to keep someone from walking off with Mech were as strict as possible. Never mind that even you  stole one there was nowhere to go that it couldn" be tracked Jaime eyes were wide with excitement and fear, but she nodded and scrabbled over t0 the door'$ security panel. Bullets thudded into the other side of the crates which stood up t0 the onslaught: risked glance over the top and saw that two guards had worked their way up to the feet of the Atlas. squat- ted down ad dropped the pistol into Erik Gray's lap_ nope You know which direction to point that thing: was taking chance_ knew: The MechWarrior might decide that risking the surgery for neural implants didn"t look s0 bad compared with meeting his end in firefight; But like said before at some point your gut instincts can make all the differ- ence_ Erik clasped comfortable grip around the Nakjama_ rose up and squeezed off two hasty shots before dropping back Just as quickly to sit with his back t0 the crates and stare at the feet of the Omni. Well, could make him deadly; but not brave_ popped up few more hasty shots of my own and then took time to reload new power pack into the laser rifle. moment later, switching out another clip_ noticed Erik was gone. Jaime continued t0 work at the door; heedless of the
10
